title: When Forming an Impression: - Ask Yourself:
subtitle: Separate Traits from Perception
description:
When forming an impression:
- Ask yourself: "Am I judging based on one good trait or the full picture?"
- Challenge assumptions: Notice if a positive quality is influencing unrelated judgments.
- Seek diverse feedback: Gather opinions from others to balance your perception.

Example: A charismatic colleague may not always be the best leader—focus on skills, not charm.

lifeOsSource.checkIn: Check in when you evaluate someone based on multiple traits, not just one standout quality.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.
faq.answerHtml: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.
faq.answerHtml: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Identify a recent interaction
howTo.summary: Choose a person you interacted with in the last 24 hours. This could be a colleague, friend, or vendor.

howTo.title: Name the standout trait
howTo.summary: Identify and articulate the single trait that most influenced your impression of this person. Keep it to one word.

howTo.title: Ask a disconfirming question
howTo.summary: Formulate one specific question that tests whether the identified trait predicts an unrelated competence.

howTo.title: Log your findings
howTo.summary: Record your observations and the outcome of your disconfirming question to track patterns over time.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to monitor your progress and insights. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/spot-the-halo-effect/.
